nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Croft
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of several places, for example in Lincolnshire, Lancashire, Herefordshire, and North Yorkshire, all named with Old English croft ‘small arable enclosure (normally adjoining a house)’, or a topographic name for someone who lived by a croft, from Middle English croft.
English: possibly in some cases a variant of   Craft   2.
Americanized form of German, Danish, Swedish, or Jewish   Kraft  . Compare   Kroft   and   Craft  .

variants.name: Craft
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English craft ‘skillful, clever, learned’.
English: habitational name from Croft in Leicestershire. See also   Croft  .
Americanized form of German, Danish, Swedish, or Jewish   Kraft  . See also   Croft  .

variants.name: Crate
meanings: English (Hampshire and Surrey): from Middle English crate ‘hurdle, basket’, or occasionally perhaps from crete ‘cradle’, probably applied as a metonymic occupational name for a maker of such articles. Alternatively, sometimes perhaps a variant of Croft 1.

variants.name: Crofts
meanings: English: variant of Croft , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Groft
meanings: English: perhaps an altered form of   Croft  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Croft was born in 1427, in Croft, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, William de Croft, was 34 and his mother, Mrs William de Croft, was 26. He had at least 1 son with Elinore Barre. He died on 29 July 1509, in Yarpole, Herefordshire, England, at the age of 82.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard de Croft est né en 1427, à Croft, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William de Croft, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Mrs William de Croft, avait 26 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Elinore Barre. Il est décédé le 29 juillet 1509, à Yarpole, Herefordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 82 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard de Croft 1427 in Croft, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater William de Croft 34 und seine Mutter Mrs William de Croft 26. Er hatte mit Elinore Barre mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 29. Juli 1509 im Alter von 82 Jahren in Yarpole, Herefordshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Croft è nato il nel 1427 nel Croft, Herefordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, William de Croft, aveva 34 anni e il suo madre, Mrs William de Croft, aveva 26 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Elinore Barre. Morì il 29 luglio 1509, nel Yarpole, Herefordshire, England, all'età di 82 anni.
